output:

STEP 2: MEDICAL & CYCLE STRATEGY
Luteal phase focus: Increased caloric needs with emphasis on magnesium-rich foods (seeds, nuts, leafy greens), complex carbohydrates (oats, sweet potato, quinoa), and protein to stabilize mood and energy. Evening meal designed for easy digestibility—light protein with easily digestible carbs, avoiding trigger foods (high FODMAP items already excluded). Sufficient healthy fats support hormone production and satiety.

STEP 2: MEDICAL & CYCLE STRATEGY
Luteal phase strategy: Increase complex carbohydrates and magnesium-rich foods (oats, nuts, seeds, leafy greens) to stabilize serotonin and manage cravings. Include adequate protein (1.2g/kg) to support satiety during higher caloric needs. Evening meal focuses on easily digestible proteins and cooked vegetables to minimize bloating; avoid raw cruciferous vegetables and excessive fiber at dinner. Distribute calories with slightly higher carb intake post-workout tolerance typical in luteal phase.

STEP 1: INGREDIENT FILTERING

STEP 2: MEDICAL & CYCLE STRATEGY
Luteal phase adaptation: Increase complex carbohydrates and magnesium-rich foods (seeds, leafy greens, nuts) to manage energy dips and mood fluctuations. Boost protein intake across all meals (target 85-90g) to support muscle recovery and satiety. Evening meal focuses on easily digestible tofu-based protein with cooked vegetables to prevent bloating; avoid raw cruciferous vegetables at dinner. Include omega-3 sources (flaxseeds, chia) for hormonal support.

STEP 3: MEAL PLAN
